scope: Reference-driven GUI/UX parity criteria for selectable product-style presets. The criteria track independent visual/workflow resemblance without using proprietary assets or user-specific data.

reference_basis: User-approved connected-session MobaXterm-style reference screenshot with ribbon, quick connect, vertical rail, docked SFTP browser, remote monitoring, SSH banner, terminal and bottom telemetry.
User-approved MobaXterm-style home reference screenshot with centered welcome title, primary/recover actions, session search, recent sessions and footer notice.

requirements.description: MobaXterm-style top titlebar uses shared static/live metadata for connected-session title text, generated app mark, compact window controls and titlebar geometry without copying proprietary assets.
MobaXterm-style top menu row uses shared static/live metadata for Terminal, Sessions, View, X server, Tools, Games, Settings, Macros and Help, with stable keys and primary actions.
Top menu and large ribbon actions cover session, server, tools, games, split, multiexec, tunneling, packages, settings, help, X server and exit affordances.

Static and live MobaXterm-style ribbons use independent drawn pictograms for session, servers, tools, games, sessions, split, multiexec, tunneling, packages, settings, help, X server and exit instead of letter placeholders or copied product assets.

scripts/check_real_gui_render.py: must use a generated ribbon icon

requirements.description: Left chrome includes quick connect plus a role-based vertical icon rail for collapse, sessions, favorites, tools, macros and SFTP.
MobaXterm-style Quick Connect top strip uses shared metadata for placeholder text, dropdown marker, fixed height and focused state across static and live evidence.
MobaXterm-style Quick Connect suggestion dropdown uses shared metadata for typed query, saved-profile match, direct SSH fallback, row density and stable live item data.

Connected MobaXterm-style reference keeps Quick Connect idle by default with placeholder text and hidden suggestions so the SFTP/monitoring dock is immediately visible; typed query suggestion evidence remains separate.

scripts/check_real_gui_render.py: connected quick-connect suggestions must stay hidden

requirements.description: Connected MobaXterm-style vertical rail includes rotated section labels for Sessions, Tools, Macros and SFTP from shared metadata, matching the reference rail structure without copied assets.
MobaXterm-style saved user sessions keep the compact user-session tree while connected sessions can switch to SFTP.
MobaXterm-style density and spacing keep the compact dark shell, wide connected dock, narrow rail and tight tab/tree spacing without user-specific samples.

Static visual metrics pin screenshot-derived MobaXterm-style connected-session boundaries for the quick-connect strip, ribbon baseline, connected dock divider, tab baseline, SFTP dock bottom rule, telemetry divider and status bar.
Static visual metrics require the connected MobaXterm-style ribbon, dock, SFTP area, terminal and telemetry zones to keep their reference order and containment.
Connected-session reference uses a wide left SFTP/monitoring dock instead of a profile-tree plus duplicate in-workspace file pane.
Live MobaXterm-style tabs switch the left dock between saved sessions and the active connected-session SFTP/monitoring view.
Connected MobaXterm-style tab strip uses shared home, inactive SSH session, active SSH session and plus-tab metadata with generated tab icons, close affordances and live tab role properties.

Connected MobaXterm-style session edge shortcut cluster tracks the small paperclip/settings controls near the tab and terminal edge with shared action metadata, generated icons and live widget properties.

Connected MobaXterm-style terminal workspace has a narrow right utility rail with generated clip, settings and tool icons instead of text placeholders, with live widget properties and static visual metrics.
SFTP browser shows toolbar actions, remote path, a selected parent-folder row, file name, size and last-modified columns.

MobaXterm-style SFTP browser path strip and file-table headers use shared static/live metadata for the remote path placeholder, dropdown marker, selected parent-folder row and keyed Name, Size and Last modified columns.

MobaXterm-style connected SFTP dock uses independent drawn toolbar, file, folder and monitoring glyphs instead of bracketed text placeholders in the static preview, while the live dock uses icon-bearing file rows and action buttons.

MobaXterm-style connected SFTP dock uses shared toolbar action metadata, remote path strip, file-table columns, monitoring metric keys and follow-terminal-folder state across static and live render evidence.

MobaXterm-style connected SFTP dock uses shared density metadata for the toolbar, path row, table header, file rows, monitoring panel and divider so static and live evidence keep the reference-like compact left-dock spacing.
MobaXterm-style SFTP toolbar actions are grouped into navigation, transfer, manage, mode and terminal clusters with reference-like separator boundaries across static and live evidence.
Remote monitoring is visible at the bottom of the left dock and is backed by SSH telemetry state.

MobaXterm-style connected left dock keeps the visible remote-monitoring area compact like the reference while retaining telemetry metric keys, refresh cadence, SSH command evidence and follow-folder SFTP plan metadata for the bottom telemetry/status surface.

MobaXterm-style connected SFTP dock exposes shared keyed controls for Remote monitoring and Follow terminal folder, with checked state, icon keys and tooltips validated in both static and live evidence.
Follow-terminal-folder control is represented in the UI and maps to the SFTP list plan for the active path.
Connected SSH session banner shows direct SSH, compression, SSH-browser and X11 state.
MobaXterm-style SSH banner chrome uses shared centered title/subtitle and static/live geometry metadata so the generated preview and live PyQt banner keep the same compact terminal placement.

MobaXterm-style SSH banner capability card exposes the target line, keyed Direct SSH, SSH compression, SSH-browser and X11-forwarding rows, row status metadata and help/website footer links across static and live evidence.

MobaXterm-style connected terminal transcript uses shared SSH/SFTP state for the web-console line, last-login line, prompt commands and health output across static preview and live PyQt terminal evidence.

Bottom connected-session status strip tracks target, CPU, RAM, disk, network, connection and process state as ordered reference-like cells with stable display text, icon keys, widths and separators across static and live evidence.

Connected MobaXterm-style chrome uses target-centric window/tab labels and icon-backed telemetry segments from shared SSH/SFTP state, using generic example targets instead of user-specific sample data.

scripts/check_real_gui_render.py: window title must be connected target label

requirements.description: MobaXterm-style window status bar exposes SFTP, CPU monitoring and SSH-browser workflow state.

MobaXterm-style bottom status bar uses shared project-owned notice text, product note, keyed workflow segments and a compact right marker across static and live evidence without copying proprietary wording.
MobaXterm-style bottom status edge includes compact previous-tab, next-tab and close-active controls from shared metadata, with generated arrows and close glyphs in static and live evidence.

MobaXterm-style home tab exposes a centered welcome surface with generated logo, primary/recover actions, session search, recent sessions and footer from shared metadata in both live GUI evidence and a dedicated static state preview rather than generic workflow cards.
MobaXterm-style interaction state tracks quick-connect focus, checked SFTP rail state and disabled sample ribbon affordance without user-specific data.

reference_basis: SecureCRT-style terminal-first operator surface with restrained dark chrome, wide tabs and session tree.

requirements.description: Preset metadata declares terminal-first density and restrained tab behavior.
Static visual metrics pin SecureCRT-style toolbar, Session Manager divider, tab baseline, command-window edge, log separator and status-bar boundary lines.
Static visual metrics require the SecureCRT-style Session Manager, terminal, command window and session log zones to keep their reference order and containment.
Renderer exposes terminal panes and command/status rows for terminal-centric operation.
North tabs and wide tab padding remain visible in preview and live metadata.
SecureCRT-style tab strip distinguishes SSH2 sessions, SFTP sessions, local shell and Start Page tabs.
Live and rendered SecureCRT-style navigation is labeled as a Session Manager for foldered terminal sessions.
SecureCRT-style Session Manager includes shared filter and action-strip chrome for Connect, New Folder and Properties workflows in static and live evidence.
SecureCRT-style tree uses a Session Database root, foldered sessions and SSH2/SFTP terminal entries.
SecureCRT-style static sidebar uses independent folder, terminal, SFTP and pinned-session glyphs for Session Manager entries instead of text-only rows.
SecureCRT-style toolbar uses session-oriented actions such as New Session, Properties, SFTP, Transfer and terminal tiling.

SecureCRT-style top menu and toolbar chrome uses shared metadata for File/Edit/View/Options/Transfer/Script/Tools/Window/Help menus plus icon-keyed session toolbar actions in static and live evidence.

docs/GUI_DESIGN.md: SecureCRT-style top menu and toolbar chrome

requirements.description: SecureCRT-style central workspace is a terminal-first Session Manager surface with SSH2, SFTP and command-window state instead of a generic split terminal.
SecureCRT-style static workspace includes a command-window strip with target scope, command input and send action for terminal-session workflows.
SecureCRT-style Command Window chrome is shared metadata and live PyQt evidence with stable keys for target scope, command input, send action and status.

SecureCRT-style session status strip uses shared static/live metadata for session, target, protocol, cipher, SFTP tab, log file and connected state so the terminal workspace carries reference-like connected-session context.
Live SecureCRT-style home tab exposes workflow evidence cards for Command Window, Session Manager and SFTP tab behavior.
SecureCRT-style reference state ties the active SSH2 tab, Session Manager sidebar, SSH/SFTP protocol state, target and status segments to one shared metadata source used by live and static evidence.
SecureCRT-style interaction state tracks Session Manager focus, active SSH2 connection, checked SFTP action and guarded delete affordance.
SecureCRT-style status bar exposes SSH2, Session Manager and active-tab state.

reference_basis: Termius-style SSH-focused dark workspace with compact hosts, vault cues and vertical session tabs.

requirements.description: Preset metadata uses west-positioned tabs to mimic a vertical session workflow.
Static visual metrics pin Termius-style toolbar, Hosts divider, west-tab rail edge, workflow-card top rule, sync activity separator and status-bar boundary lines.
Static visual metrics require the Termius-style host sidebar, west tab rail, workspace, workflow cards and sync activity zones to keep their reference order and containment.
Termius-style west tab rail distinguishes SSH host, files, vault and new-session actions.
Renderer shows SSH terminal sessions and vault/status workflow cues.
Preset metadata declares compact host sidebar and dark SSH-focused description.
Live and rendered Termius-style navigation is labeled as Hosts with vault-backed SSH inventory language.
Termius-style tree uses a Personal Vault root with host, team and snippet groups.
Termius-style static sidebar uses independent vault, host and snippet glyphs for Personal and Teams inventory rows.
Termius-style Hosts sidebar exposes a dedicated search field and Add Host, Keychain and Sync actions, with host-search focus measured against that sidebar field.
Termius-style toolbar uses SSH-host workflow actions such as Sync, New Host, Port Fwd, Snippet and Vault.
Termius-style central workspace is a host/vault surface with SSH host, files, snippets and port-forward state.
Termius-style static workspace includes vault identity, port-forward and snippet workflow cards below the active host terminal.
Termius-style host header chips are shared static/live metadata with stable keys for vault, sync and port-forward state.
Termius-style workspace exposes host, vault identity, jump chain, SFTP, port-forward, snippet and sync state as a shared static/live identity strip.
Live Termius-style home tab exposes workflow evidence cards for vault identity, port forwarding and snippets.
Termius-style reference state ties the active host tab, Hosts sidebar, SSH/vault protocol state, target and status segments to one shared metadata source used by live and static evidence.
Termius-style interaction state tracks host-search focus, active connection, checked vault action and guarded remove action.
Termius-style status bar exposes vault, port-forwarding and sync state.

reference_basis: Remmina-style light remote-desktop workspace with protocol profiles and viewer tabs.

requirements.description: Preset metadata declares a light remote-desktop workspace with clear protocol profiles.
Static visual metrics pin Remmina-style toolbar, profile/workspace divider, viewer tab baseline, remote viewer frame and connection-activity separator lines.
Static visual metrics require the Remmina-style protocol profiles, viewer controls, remote viewer, options pane and connection activity zones to keep their reference order.
Profile tree and renderer include RDP/VNC/SFTP-style protocol targets.
Live and rendered Remmina-style navigation is labeled as Connection Profiles with remote-desktop protocol coverage.

src/remote_ops_workspace/gui_designs.py: RDP, VNC, SSH and SFTP targets

requirements.description: Remmina-style tree groups connection profiles by RDP, VNC and SSH/SFTP protocol families.
Remmina-style static sidebar uses independent protocol glyphs for RDP, VNC and SSH/SFTP profile rows.
Remmina-style toolbar uses remote-desktop profile and viewer actions such as New Profile, Shared, Transfer, Prefs and tiled viewers.
Remmina-style status bar exposes remote desktop protocol readiness, scaling and clipboard state.
Remmina-style tab strip distinguishes RDP, VNC, SFTP and Quick Connect viewer tabs.
Remmina-style central workspace is a remote desktop viewer with scaling, clipboard, fullscreen and profile-option controls.
Remmina-style static viewer contains protocol-aware control glyphs for fit, scale, clipboard, fullscreen and screenshots plus visible RDP connection state cards.
Remmina-style viewer controls are shared static/live metadata with stable keys and icon keys for Fit, Scale 100%, Clipboard, Fullscreen and Screenshot actions.
Remmina-style profile manager exposes a filterable connection list with stable Name, Protocol and Server columns plus selected RDP/VNC/SFTP rows.
Live Remmina-style home tab exposes workflow evidence cards for protocol viewer tabs, scaling controls and clipboard sync.
Remmina-style reference state ties the active RDP viewer tab, Connection Profiles sidebar, RDP target, scale state and status segments to one shared metadata source used by live and static evidence.
Remmina-style interaction state tracks profile-filter focus, active viewer connection, checked transfer action and guarded delete affordance.
Remmina profile import remains covered for real migration workflows.

reference_basis: mRemoteNG-style classic connection manager with persistent tree, document tabs and grouped sessions.

requirements.description: Preset metadata declares classic density, persistent tree width and document tabs.
Static visual metrics pin mRemoteNG-style toolbar, Connections divider, document tab baseline, document-control strip, config grid and connection-log separator lines.
Static visual metrics require the mRemoteNG-style connection tree, document workspace, document controls, config editor and connection log zones to keep their reference order and containment.
mRemoteNG-style tree uses a Connections root, nested containers and bracketed protocol connection nodes.
mRemoteNG-style static sidebar uses independent database, folder, SSH, RDP and SFTP glyphs for the nested connection tree.
Live and rendered mRemoteNG-style navigation is labeled as Connections with nested group language.
mRemoteNG-style toolbar uses connection-manager actions such as New Conn, Config, Open, External, Script and pane tiling.
mRemoteNG-style top menu and toolbar chrome uses shared metadata for File/View/Connections/Tools/Window/Help menus plus icon-keyed connection-manager toolbar actions in static and live evidence.

docs/GUI_DESIGN.md: mRemoteNG-style top menu and toolbar chrome

requirements.description: mRemoteNG-style status bar exposes connection file, inheritance and open-pane state.
Preset keeps north document tabs and connection-manager workspace rendering.
mRemoteNG-style document tabs distinguish SSH/RDP documents, external SFTP and Start Page.
mRemoteNG-style central workspace is a document connection surface with SSH/RDP panes, external SFTP and inheritance/config state.
mRemoteNG-style static workspace includes a document-manager toolbar, embedded RDP control glyphs and a grid-like config/inheritance editor.
mRemoteNG-style document toolbar chrome is shared static/live metadata with stable keys for Connections.xml, Save, Reconnect, External tool, Dock view and the connection-tree filter.
mRemoteNG-style property and inheritance grid is shared static/live metadata with stable columns, inherited row state, effective values and source paths.
Live mRemoteNG-style home tab exposes workflow evidence cards for document toolbar, RDP document and config inheritance behavior.

mRemoteNG-style reference state ties the active SSH document tab, Connections sidebar, target, inherited document state and status segments to one shared metadata source used by live and static evidence.
mRemoteNG-style interaction state tracks connection-tree focus, active document tab, checked external-tool action and guarded delete affordance.
mRemoteNG import maps nested groups and connection tree data.
